rulings.Case Title: RE SOCORRO S. ARTATES -TRUST 1992
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition Concerning Internal Affairs Of The Trust
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Matter set for hearing for the court to determine how to proceed. 2) At the prior hearing the judicial officer stated there are no allegations as to why venue is proper the fact the property is in SF is not sufficient. A Supplement filed 5/28/2021 alleges venue is San Francisco because the trust is being administered in the city and county on San Francisco. No legal authority cited. 3) Petitioner requests a. Petitioners be confirmed as trustee. b. Respondents account from August 1, 1999 through present. c. John Artates pay market rate for his tenancy at Chenery Street Property. d. Trustees include the issue of all of settlor's deceased children as income distributes. e. The agreement of at least 4 of settler's children or issue of deceased children be required regarding management of Chenery Street. f. Trust Estate be reimbursed for any expenditures found to be improper or income which cannot be properly accounted for.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VERONICA ELAINE LI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/31/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: A petition for distribution must list and describe in detail the property to be distributed, including the complete legal description, street address (if any), and assessor's parcel number (if any) of real property. [CRC 7.651.] There is no need to show a summary of account or refer to (nonexistent) schedules when the accounting was waived. There was also reportedly no cash in the estate to be invested in interest-bearing accounts A courtesy copy of a supplement, signed and verified by the administrator, providing the full legal description of the real property must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 8/20/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B. (The courtesy copies for the original hearing on 6/15/21 had been sent to another Examiner. Examiners are not singly assigned to cases; each new petition is assigned anew.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDNA MARKETON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/8/2021. 1) Please explain the petition page 3 line 17 that states "The assets on hand will be outline in a subsequent amendment." Please explain as no subsequent amendment has been filed. Please explain why an amended petition was filed knowing a subsequent amendment would need to be filed. 2) Please file a memorandum of points and authority in support of distribution from the estate to Diane Scalzio as Successor Trustee of the Gladys E. Kottschade Holm Revocable Trust when no probate estate has been opened and no personal representative appointed. It appears this must be amended and cannot be distributed to the personal representative until certified copy of letters of the personal representative have been filed. 3) Please provide a legal basis for the court ordering distribution when no personal representative has been appointed in the Estate of LaVilla Marketon. It appears this must be amended and cannot be distributed to the personal representative until certified copy of letters of the personal representative have been filed. 4) Please explain why the petition alleges an assignment to a third party was made by Joanne Gertrude Marketon when the assignment on file is for Joanna G. Hammon. Please explain how the assignment of Joanna G. Hammon and Robert F. Corcoran are effective under Probate Code 11604.5 (d). These assignments do not appear to be valid. 5) Please explain why the estate requests a $30,000.00 reserve for international transferring of the assets when the list of beneficiaries addresses are all in the USA. 6) The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. [LRSF 14.35 D.] 7) The caption of the order must begin with Order for Final Distribution. [LRSF 14.36 I. 6) b.] 8) The last page of the petition line 10 and page 5 line 6 of the proposed order states "fund shall be distributed accordingly to paragraph 32 of this prayer without further order of" There is no paragraph 32 in the "prayer" The omnibus clause should state specifically how the estate is to be distributed and to whom not just cite a paragraph #. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 8/23/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues may include (but not limited to): 1) The conservator requests authority to sell the conservatee's former residence without publication of notice of sale; and 2) The conservator requests authority to create the conservatee's trust and pour-over will by substituted judgment. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARSHA FLORINE MONTGOMERY-LOCKETT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/14/2021. 1) Neither the petition for probate nor the supplements alleged Decedent had pre-deceased siblings. Please state the date of death for Decedent's mother Hazel Olcutt, the name and date of death name for the other parent, and the date of death for predeceased siblings Vernon Alan Tolliver and Edward Lane Tilliver Jr. Please explain whether the other parent has any issue. Please explain whether pre-deceased siblings Vernon or Edward have any issue. (CRC 7.103). if there are any issue they must receive notice. (Probate Code 1202). 2) Please file a receipt showing $825.00 fee was paid to the probate referee. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 8/30/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CRISTINA R. VALDIVIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/24/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. Was Notice of Proposed Action given to sell the real property? The calculation of the statutory compensation was slightly off. Did the administrator and his attorneys wish to waive the difference or amend the petition to request the additional $599.40? An amendment will require notice. [CRC 7.53(a).] Exhibit E (attorney costs advanced) show two costs for publication but only one proof of publication was filed. Why was republication necessary? The Probate Referee's commission shown in the I&A filed on 1/14/21 was $1,400, not $1,375. Was she fully paid? (It would have been helpful if Exhibit F had included the description or purpose of each disbursement or if Exhibit G had corresponded to the order in which those disbursements were listed in Exhibit F.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 8/13/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 8/30/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WOON FUNG CHAN REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions And Confirming Trust Asset
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/31/21. No courtesy copies. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C. ] No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A.] No proof of service. A quick skim of the petition does not show that all vested or contingent beneficiaries of the trust and their last known addresses were set forth. [CRC 7.902.] Notice with a copy of any supplement to the petition must be served. [CRC 7.53(b).]
